 {"id":"bd-jrgbe","title":"Add golden formatting contract for representative ffs-types scalar displays and parse errors","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T21:35:39.800324358Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T21:44:13.722101004Z","closed_at":"2026-04-18T21:44:13.719925425Z","close_reason":"Added exact golden formatting contract for representative ffs-types scalar displays and ParseError messages; verified with rch cargo test -p ffs-types representative_scalar_and_parse_error_exact_golden_contract -- --nocapture, rch cargo check --workspace --all-targets, rch cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings, and rch cargo fmt --check","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-jtt2","title":"Implement external journal paired replay (not just UUID validation)","description":"BACKGROUND: ext4 filesystems can use an external journal device (JOURNAL_DEV incompat flag on the data FS, journal_dev field points to device number, journal_uuid links the pair). When crash recovery is needed, the JBD2 journal blocks live on the external device rather than in an internal inode.

CURRENT STATE: OpenOptions::external_journal_path accepts an external journal image path. maybe_probe_external_journal opens it, reads its JBD2 superblock, validates UUID pairing and block size match, and stores ExternalJournalInfo on OpenFs. But maybe_replay_ext4_journal short-circuits when journal_dev != 0 — it logs 'journal replay skipped' and returns Ok(None). No actual replay occurs.

The log message honestly states: 'external journal UUID validation only; full paired replay not yet implemented'.

IMPLEMENTATION PLAN:
1. When journal_dev != 0 AND external_journal_path is Some AND UUIDs match:
   a. Open the external journal image as a ByteDevice.
   b. Parse the JBD2 superblock from the external device.
   c. Compute journal segments: the entire external device (minus superblock) is the journal area.
   d. Feed the segments to replay_jbd2_segments the same way we do for internal journals.
   e. The replay writes go to the DATA filesystem's device, not the journal device.
2. When journal_dev != 0 AND external_journal_path is None AND crash recovery IS needed:
   - Currently returns UnsupportedFeature error (good — fail-fast).
   - No change needed here.
3. When journal_dev != 0 AND external_journal_path is None AND crash recovery NOT needed:
   - Currently returns Ok(None) with info log (good — graceful skip).
   - No change needed here.

KEY CONSIDERATIONS:
- The external journal's block layout is different from an internal journal. The JBD2 superblock is at block 0 of the device, and journal blocks start immediately after. There's no inode or extent mapping.
- We need to compute JournalSegment { start, blocks } for the external device based on the JBD2 superblock's s_maxlen field.
- The replay must write to the data device, not the journal device. The existing replay infrastructure takes a BlockDevice for writes — we just need to pass the data device.
- After successful replay, we should clear the needs_recovery flag on the data device's superblock.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- A test creates a synthetic data FS + external journal pair, dirties the journal, and verifies replay applies blocks to the data device.
- The log message changes from 'UUID validation only' to 'external journal replay completed'.
- UUID mismatch still fails fast.
- Block size mismatch still fails fast.
- ExternalJournalInfo is populated with journal metadata after successful replay.
- The 'required crash recovery' error path still works when no external path is provided.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:05:40.642853327Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-25T03:42:00.763994355Z","closed_at":"2026-03-25T03:42:00.763527321Z","close_reason":"All subtasks complete. External journal paired replay is fully implemented: UUID validation, segment computation (bd-vdk6), and replay wiring (bd-vql1) are all done. 4 tests covering success path, UUID mismatch, dirty FS requirement, and clean FS skip.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ext4","journal","journal_dev"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-jtt2","depends_on_id":"bd-vdk6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:10:55.294301302Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-jtt2","depends_on_id":"bd-vql1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-25T03:10:56.821268230Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-juy7","title":"Add ext4 inode checksum adversarial fuzz regression coverage","description":"Fallback testing-fuzzing parity round: add deterministic adversarial corpus coverage for ext4 inode checksum stamping/verification, corruption rejection, and size-boundary failures in crates/ffs-ondisk/tests/adversarial_corpus.rs; update conformance/COVERAGE.md.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T06:36:09.623765671Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T06:51:33.215554859Z","closed_at":"2026-04-18T06:51:33.213412544Z","close_reason":"Added ext4 inode checksum adversarial fuzz regression coverage","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ext4","fuzz","ondisk","testing"]}
+{"id":"bd-jzxq1","title":"[HIGH] fuzz_ext4_checksums fails to compile - API drift","description":"Fuzz target fuzz/fuzz_targets/fuzz_ext4_checksums.rs:26 calls verify_group_desc_checksum with wrong signature. Function at crates/ffs-ondisk/src/ext4.rs:1735 signature changed but fuzz target not updated. Current call: verify_group_desc_checksum(data, sb.checksum_seed, 0, 64). Missing arguments: uuid (&[u8; 16]), desc_size (u16), checksum_kind (Ext4GroupDescChecksumKind).","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-22T21:24:19.859993342Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T21:24:19.859993342Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-k1r7x","title":"revalidate stale xfstests ioctl allowlist entries after FIEMAP and SETFLAGS coverage landed","description":"Survey finding: `scripts/e2e/xfstests_allowlist.json` still says `ext4/001` is blocked because FUSE does not forward `FIEMAP`, and `ext4/005` is blocked because inode flag manipulation is unsupported, but `FEATURE_PARITY.md` and `crates/ffs-harness/tests/fuse_e2e.rs` now prove mounted-path `FIEMAP` and `EXT4_IOC_GETFLAGS` / `EXT4_IOC_SETFLAGS` coverage.

Scope:
- Re-run the relevant xfstests or close analogues against the current FUSE path.
- Replace stale allowlist reasons with the actual remaining blocker, if any, instead of the old pre-coverage assumption.
- Update docs/notes so xfstests triage matches current FrankenFS ioctl behavior.
- Keep explicit separation between transport-layer kernel limits and missing FrankenFS behavior.

Acceptance:
- `ext4/001` and `ext4/005` have current, evidence-backed allowlist notes or are removed from the allowlist.
- No xfstests note claims missing FIEMAP or inode-flag support when the repo already proves that surface.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T03:16:34.125296714Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T06:52:36.236299402Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T06:52:36.234126017Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","ext4","fuse","ioctl","xfstests"]}
 {"id":"bd-k2n3","title":"Refresh stale README test-count claims","description":"README.md still advertises 3591 tests in the badge and install/gate section. Verify the current workspace test inventory through rch and update the public count so docs match the current tree.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-05T23:33:28.642261452Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-05T23:37:08.446751783Z","closed_at":"2026-04-05T23:37:08.446360171Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["docs","parity","readme"]}
 {"id":"bd-k2xhy","title":"add mounted-path ext4 read-only EROFS coverage for fallocate mutation attempts","description":"Survey finding: ext4 fuse_e2e covers writable fallocate success and invalid/unsupported mode rejection, but not the mounted-path read-only contract even though ffs-fuse rejects fallocate with EROFS before dispatch. Scope: add ext4 read-only probes that attempt representative fallocate mutations such as preallocate and keep-size or punch-hole forms, asserting exact EROFS with unchanged apparent size and file contents. Acceptance: ext4 FUSE E2E freezes operator-visible read-only refusal for fallocate mutation attempts.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T08:33:02.558777625Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T21:01:08.231928562Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T21:01:08.230039713Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-r7od","title":"Add generic/112 preallocation conformance coverage for ext4 and btrfs","description":"Upstream xfstests generic/112 runs fsx with -A and -x to exercise AIO plus preallocated unwritten extents. FrankenFS already has fallocate tests, but the harness lacks an explicit cross-filesystem conformance check for the preallocation contract that generic/112 depends on. Add deterministic ext4+btrfs harness coverage proving that preallocation extends size, unwritten regions read as zeroes, writes into preallocated space preserve surrounding zero-fill, and the contract survives reopen.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T17:23:32.225284126Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T18:00:09.368172019Z","closed_at":"2026-04-18T18:00:09.365241100Z","close_reason":"Added generic/112 preallocation conformance coverage for ext4 and btrfs via shared harness assertions","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-r7vx0","title":"[HIGH] restore missing fuzz_ioctl_dispatch corpus dir for crash promotion pipeline","description":"Workspace gate regression from `cargo test --workspace --all-targets` on 2026-04-22 via `rch` worker `vmi1156319`:

- `crash_promotion::tests::corpus_directories_exist` panics because `fuzz/corpus/fuzz_ioctl_dispatch` is missing
- `crash_promotion::tests::pipeline_validation_passes` then fails with `22/23 corpus directories found`

Root cause is repo drift between the fuzz target inventory and the shipped corpus directories. Acceptance:
1. `fuzz/corpus/fuzz_ioctl_dispatch/` exists with at least one seed or the pipeline no longer requires it.
2. `crates/ffs-harness/src/crash_promotion.rs` inventory and repo layout are consistent.
3. `cargo test -p ffs-harness crash_promotion -- --nocapture` passes via `rch`.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-22T12:45:08.560673853Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T12:45:08.560673853Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"bd-r8c8r","title":"[HIGH] fixture checksum manifest drifted for ext4 inline-data continuation JSON","description":"Reproduced from `env AGENT_NAME=cod-fs CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_frankenfs_cod rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_frankenfs_cod cargo test --workspace --all-targets` on 2026-04-22.

Failure:
- `fixture_checksum_manifest_is_complete` at `crates/ffs-harness/tests/conformance.rs:3680`
- fixture: `ext4_inode_inline_data_with_continuation.json`
- manifest digest: `b040217daafc0d5a7706a9263652b33776989f5b7c5c7f76e807a243860f6218`
- actual digest: `5f811167369ea9e1bee12196a8ce72f070cd08105d85473401e22dc28a646525`

The fixture content and checksum manifest are out of sync, so the conformance gate stays red even when parser behavior is otherwise stable.

Acceptance:
- fixture and checksum manifest agree again
- `fixture_checksum_manifest_is_complete` passes
- `full_conformance_gate_pass` is no longer blocked by this manifest slice.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T18:25:18.016914474Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T21:19:07.491927192Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","ext4","fixtures","review"]}

Acceptance:

- `fixture_checksum_manifest_is_complete` passes
- `full_conformance_gate_pass` is no longer blocked by this manifest slice.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T18:25:18.016914474Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T21:24:27.000000000Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T21:24:27.000000000Z","closed_reason":"already shipped","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","ext4","fixtures","review"]}
 {"id":"bd-r9n2u","title":"add ffs-core fuzz target for path-component validation","description":"Apply /testing-fuzzing to crates/ffs-core/src/lib.rs::validate_single_path_component(name: &[u8]). Scope: add a very high-throughput libFuzzer target over raw path bytes, seeded with valid ASCII names plus boundary cases for empty, dot, dot-dot, slash-containing, NUL-containing, overlong, and non-UTF8 names. Assert totality (no panics), deterministic errno-class mapping, and compatibility with the callers that use this gate for create, link, symlink, rename, and xattr-adjacent path mutations. Acceptance: new fuzz target registered in fuzz/Cargo.toml, corpus seeds added, and any minimized crashers promoted to regression tests.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-22T05:59:00Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T11:06:57Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T11:06:57Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ffs-core","fuzz","paths","testing","validation"]}
 {"id":"bd-raks","title":"Fix missing FsGeometry fields in ffs-inode tests","description":"## Purpose

Restore workspace compilation by updating stale `FsGeometry` initializers in `ffs-inode` to include the required `desc_size` and `feature_ro_compat` fields.

## Scope

1. Reproduce the current `cargo check --workspace --all-targets` failure via `rch`.
2. Patch the failing `FsGeometry` initializers in `crates/ffs-inode/src/lib.rs`.
3. Re-run targeted `rch` checks for `ffs-inode` and, if clean, the workspace check.

## Acceptance criteria
- `crates/ffs-inode/src/lib.rs` compiles with current `FsGeometry` definition
- Targeted `rch exec -- cargo check -p ffs-inode --all-targets` passes
- Workspace check moves past this failure or reports the next unrelated blocker
","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-03-20T03:40:47.448066803Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-20T20:09:05.994009750Z","closed_at":"2026-03-20T20:09:05.993264249Z","close_reason":"Patched missing FsGeometry fields in ffs-inode tests; targeted rch cargo check -p ffs-inode --all-targets passed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["build","inode","testing"]}
 {"id":"bd-rale","title":"Improve FfsError::Io errno mapping with ErrorKind fallback","description":"When std::io::Error has no raw_os_error() (e.g. created via Error::new()), to_errno() falls back to EIO. Improve by mapping io::ErrorKind to appropriate errnos (NotFound→ENOENT, PermissionDenied→EACCES, etc.) before falling back to EIO. This gives FUSE callers more specific error codes.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-26T00:49:02.204994325Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-26T00:59:08.073573327Z","closed_at":"2026-02-26T00:59:08.073546988Z","close_reason":"Improved to_errno() Io variant: now maps ErrorKind to specific errnos (NotFound→ENOENT, PermissionDenied→EACCES, AlreadyExists→EEXIST, WouldBlock→EAGAIN, InvalidInput/InvalidData→EINVAL, TimedOut→ETIMEDOUT, Interrupted→EINTR) when raw_os_error() is None, instead of always falling back to EIO. Added test io_error_kind_mapping_without_raw_errno covering all mapped kinds. Also fixed two pre-existing clippy pedantic lints. All 20 tests pass, clippy clean.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
